reasoning: According to Wikipedia as of October 2025, Ariarne Titmus holds the long-course 200m freestyle world record (1:52.23), set on 12 June 2024. The 2025 World Aquatics Championships concluded in August 2025 without the record being broken (Mollie O'Callaghan won with a time of 1:53.48). Titmus recently announced her retirement in October 2025, which eliminates the possibility of her lowering the record herself and leaves only a few active threats (namely O'Callaghan and Summer McIntosh). The forecast window covers November 2025 to January 2026, which is primarily the international short-course season. Although Australia's domestic summer sees some long-course state championships in December, elite swimmers rarely taper for world-record peak performances during these meets. Combined with the very high base rate (0.991) indicating stability of swimming world records within narrow timeframes, the likelihood of an unratified record appearing or the record being broken before January 2026 is exceptionally low. Given platform bounds, the probabilities are placed at the structural maximum for near-certain events (0.95) and stepped down appropriately as the forecast horizon extends to January, reflecting standard decay of confidence over time and the marginal risk of a surprise swim.
